style: Calm, technical Home Assistant night readout. The hook is the typographic hero; one current-stage card carries neutral stage labels and a fixed amber estimate qualifier. Solid surfaces, thin borders, no gradients, no decorative motion.

motion.dominant_motif: A deterministic vertical fade-and-settle between pre-authored current-stage labels.

timeline.action: Hold the complete hook, Current stage label, em-dash value, and attached sensor estimate chip. No entrance motion.
Fade and move the em dash upward, then settle the pre-authored REM label from below.
Move REM upward, then settle the first pre-authored Light sleep label from below.
Move Light sleep upward, then settle the pre-authored Deep sleep label from below.
Move Deep sleep upward, then settle the final pre-authored Light sleep label; complete the payoff by 3.0s.
Hold Current stage: Light sleep with the sensor estimate chip fixed and attached.
Retrace Light sleep to Deep sleep to Light sleep to REM to the em dash using inverse vertical fade-and-settle motion.
Hold the exact opening CSS transform and opacity state.

constraints: No audio, video, gradients, camera movement, or decorative effects.
No timeline, duration, average, night summary, session summary, or numeric telemetry appears on-screen.
No stage value uses semantic color; all stage values are neutral white.
The amber estimate qualifier is always explicit text inside a fixed outline.
Only pre-authored labels animate; no per-frame text mutation or nondeterministic logic.
The composition remains one self-contained six-second shot.
